summary: Make chunk_ids and corpus directory names internally consistent (root cause: chunk.py uses display name, dirs are snake_case)

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Root cause: scripts/chunk.py:191 builds chunk_id = f'{tradition_val}.{source_id}.{idx + 1:03d}' where tradition_val comes from meta.toml display name ('Christian Mysticism'), but the on-disk path is CORPUS_DIR / tradition (the snake_case dir name). They diverge for every multi-word or capitalized tradition. Already-lowercase traditions (gnosticism, hermeticism, jewish_mysticism) work by accident.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Pick one canonical form (recommend the directory name — already snake_case, URL-safe, machine-readable). Update chunk.py:191 to use the dir-name var. Backfill: rewrite chunk.id field in every corpus/*/*/chunks/*.toml, then re-run scripts/graph_bootstrap.py and re-embed (scripts/embed_corpus.py) to rebuild nodes/edges/staged_tags/staged_edges/vector_store with the new ids. Keep tradition display name as a separate field on chunk_data['chunk']['tradition'] (already there). After this lands, remove the fallback branch from the helper introduced by 6ccb04d9.
